items.title: USDA final rule raises SNAP retailer stocking standards; effective July 7 with Nov. 4 compliance deadline
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service finalized updated SNAP retailer staple food stocking standards that take effect July 7, 2026, and require retailers to comply no later than Nov. 4, 2026. The rule implements the 2014 Farm Bill’s increase from 3 to 7 distinct staple food varieties in each of the 4 staple food categories and increases the number of categories requiring at least one perishable variety from 2 to 3.

items.title: KFF: States preparing for Oct. 1, 2026 Medicaid/CHIP eligibility restrictions for some lawfully residing immigrants
items.summary: KFF reports that beginning Oct. 1, 2026, Medicaid eligibility for immigrants will be restricted to lawful permanent residents, certain Haitian and Cuban entrants, and Compact of Free Association (COFA) migrants, while some other lawfully residing groups (including asylees and refugees) will lose eligibility. More than half of states (28) said they plan to send 1–3 advance notices 30 to 90+ days before the change, and 22 states plan to engage community-based organizations for outreach.

items.title: USDA posts WIC income eligibility guidelines for 2026–27 (effective July 1)
items.summary: USDA published WIC income eligibility guidelines effective July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027. For the 48 contiguous states and D.C., the 100% federal poverty guideline for a family of 4 is $33,000/year ($2,750/month), and the 185% level used for reduced-price meals is $61,050/year ($5,088/month).

items.title: FSA reminds schools about APP-26-03 FAFSA real-time fraud detection (updated May 14)
items.summary: Federal Student Aid’s ‘What’s New’ page highlights Electronic Announcement APP-26-03 on FAFSA real-time fraud detection, updated May 14, 2026. Students and families may see additional identity-verification steps during online FAFSA completion as the Department continues its fraud-prevention measures.

items.title: HUD wind-down ends Restore-Rebuild (Faircloth-to-RAD) for new deals; key cutoff May 12
items.summary: HUD issued a May 12, 2026 memorandum winding down the Restore-Rebuild initiative (formerly Faircloth-to-RAD), making projects ineligible if a NARR request was not submitted before May 12. HUD will continue processing NARR requests submitted before May 12, but any issued NARR becomes void 90 days after the memo date (Aug. 10, 2026) unless specific advancement milestones are met.

items.title: IRS reminder: extension to file is available until Oct. 15, 2026 (but must be requested by April 15)
items.summary: The IRS reminds taxpayers that a filing extension gives extra time to file a federal return until Oct. 15, 2026. However, the extension must be requested by April 15, 2026 to avoid late-filing penalties, and any tax owed is still due by the original deadline.

items.title: SSA: May Social Security payments follow staggered schedule; SSI typically paid on the 1st
items.summary: Social Security benefits are paid on a staggered schedule tied to birth dates (second, third, or fourth Wednesday), while Supplemental Security Income (SSI) is generally paid on the first of the month. For May 2026, payment dates fall on May 13, May 20, and May 27 for many retirement and SSDI beneficiaries depending on birth date.
